entry.title: S/4Hana Brownfield Conversion Project
entry.content:
First, the system needs to be prepared, that is, the ECC system is moved to the S4 HANA structure by technical consultants (BASIS-ABAP). For this, they upload the relevant sap notes to the system and create a sandbox system. All module consultants provide testing and controls in this test system, then if necessary, the 2nd sandbox system can be installed as a development system or directly to the QA system, then the live system will be switched.

Throughout the project you will hear the following terms very often SI Check List and SI items

SI: Simplification Item.

Technical consultants run Simplification Item Check Report and if there is an error or inconsistency, they assign it to the relevant module consultants.

All module consultants check their own inconsistencies and errors and make the necessary corrections.

go to se38 /SDF/RC_START_CHECK

You can check SI ıtem and sap notes in this report

SAP Readiness Check Cloud Application

ZIP files are created and uploaded to the SAP Readiness Check Cloud App. The data here can be read by consultants and verified before being uploaded to the application.

For more information on this part, see this video published by SAP

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/SAAP/SAP_Activate/RC_Version_3.mp4

After this technical transition, module consultants start working on the transaction codes, tables, structures that change with S4 Hana in the sandbox system.

The most important topics are as follows;

-CVI integration, also called BP Configuration

-Transfer of price and credit master and credit management and control of the changing structure

For example, additional processing is required for Condition Master Data mappings because the data will now be used in the PRCD_ELEMENTS table instead of the KONV table. For more detailed information you can take a look at this note

https://me.sap.com/notes/2267308

If there is a Rebate Process, the Settlement management- conditon contract structure that comes with S/4 Hana can be recommended or not configured

If there are licensed products such as DSD, Aatp, etc., their licenses will be renewed and system connections will be made so that they can be used in S/4. For more detailed information, you can take a look at these notes

Aatp

https://me.sap.com/notes/2267745

https://me.sap.com/notes/2209696

Rebate

https://me.sap.com/notes/2267377/E

Except for these

Some data model simplifications have been implemented for the SD field. Removed status tables VBUK-VBUP instead status fields have been moved to tables VBAK and VBAP for orders, LIKP and LIPS for deliveries, VBRK for billing documents for more details https://me.sap.com/notes/2267306

Instead of S066, S067 tables used in Credit Management, UKMBP_CMS_SGM & UKMBP_CMS tables came in S/4 Hana. For more details https://me.sap.com/notes/2270544

For Customer master data, KNA1, KNB1, KNVV, KNVP tables were used in ECC System. BP master data will be created in S/4 Hana, BUT000-BP: General data I BUT100-BP: Roles tables will be used.

Apart from these, there are CVI_LINK tables. Customer-BP and Vendor-BP information is kept in these tables. When the numbers do not match, it can check through these.

Customer-BP table V_CVI_CUST_LINK

BP and Supplier table V_CVI_VEND_LINK

I would be happy to read your thoughts and comments and if there are better ways to fulfil the requirement, please leave them in the comment section below.

 

Thanks so much

entry.title: Sales Promotions & Sales Deals Configurations and Applying Multiple Deals to an Order
entry.content:
Before starting the customizations of Promotions & Sales Deals, we need to make sure that we have 2 condition types to use for it.

First one, Sales Deal Determining Condition Type.

Which has condition class “H”(Determining Sales Deal). And the Calculation Type should be “G” Formula.

This condition type will not directly affect the pricing. It will just help to find the promotions.

 

The other condition type is regular discount condition type.

 

Access Sequence

For Promotion Discount cond. Type, you can use material based fields. (In Order to use multiple promotions for the same customer/materials, acces type should be “A”.)

 

For Determining cond. Type, you can use customer based fields. Access Type “B” should be used.

 

 

 

Customizing Promotions & Sales Deals

SPRO Path

IMG > Sales and Distribution> Basic Functions> Pricing> Pricing Agreements

 

 

1. Define Promotions

 

You can either use the standart promotion or create new one.

 

 

You can make changes on Proposed dates and text determination fields. The other fields are not changeable.

 

 

 

2. Define Sales Deals

 

 

Click “New Entries”.

 

Now in addition to Proposed Dates, we have Control area on this screen.

 

 

 

2.1- Condition Type Group: Specifies a grouping of condition types and condition tables that will be used for the sales deals. We will configure on the next steps.

 

2.2- Determines if the condition records and the Sales Deal have the same validity dates.

If the condition records may have a different validity date from the agreement, select this field.

 

2.3- Determines if the Sales Deal should have a higher-level agreement such as Promotions.

 

3. Defining Condition Type Groups

              

               You can create new condition type group by clicking “New Entries”

 

After entering the condition type group name and definition, you need to select Condition Type group category as “A” which is Price Agreements. So incompatible condition types can not be assigned to this group. (Such as Rebate Condition types).

 

 

 

 

 

4. Assign Condition Types/Tables to Condition group

You can click “New Entries” button and assign both condition types and table numbers to condition type group accordingly.

 

5. Assign Condition Type Group to Sales Deal

After creating new sales deal, it will automatically be created on this table as well. You can enter your condition type group and save.

 

 

Customizations are done. Now we will create promotions and sales deals

 

Go to VB31, select the promotion type and enter.

 

 

Write the Sales Organization, Distribution Channel and Division and enter.

 

 

You can determine the validity period on this screen. Define the description and save.

 

Promotion 90000010 created.

 

 

Now we will create a Sales Deal.

Go to VB21.

Write the Sales Deal type and enter.

 

 

 

 

Define the Sales Deal description, validity period, currency and reference promotion number. Then click “Conditions” button.

 

 

 

The condition type pop-up screen will appear. Select one of them and create condition record.

 

Enter the customer and go back.

 

You will see the number of conditions increased. Now select the other condition type.

 

Now you can enter the material and the amount of discount. Then you can save.

 

 

 

 

 

When you create an Order for related customer and material, we can see the discount has been applied.

 

 

 

On the pricing analysis, we can see the sales deal number.

 

 

On VBAP table, we can see the sales deal and promotion numbers.

KNUMA_PI : Promotion

KNUMA_AG: Sales Deal

 

 

 

On the item level of the invoice, we can see the promotion and sales deal number.

 

 

 

If you would like to apply multiple promotions to the same material.

You need to apply the requirement “62” to promotion discount condition type on the pricing procedure.

And the type of Access sequence of the condition type should be “A”.

 

 

Then multiple Sales Deals will be applied.

I hope these informations were useful. I would love to read your thoughts and comments.

Thank you 😊

entry.title: Automatic Invoice creation for EWM related Deliveries
entry.content:
Backend System – SAP S/4HANA 2020FP02

Overview  -

This blog offers comprehensive guidance on implementing automatic invoicing for scenarios related to Extended Warehouse Management (EWM).

Business Scenario

Initially, the business was using storage location-based stock management, incorporating automatic invoicing through a custom solution at the point of goods issue for the delivery document. However, with the implementation of Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), this enhancement extension failed to function as intended, hence, the real-time invoice creation process was interrupted. We were unable to identify an appropriate user exit to implement this enhancement. Consequently, the Goods Issue status of the delivery was not completed when the existing enhancement for the invoice creation program was executed, resulting in the failure of invoice creation.

The VF04 background schedule was proposed to the business. However, the business did not agree due to potential delays in invoice creation and the need to identify the invoice creator as the same user who performed the PGI. Using VF04 would result in the invoice creator being marked as the background user.

 

 

Step 1

In order to maintain output condition records based on the warehouse number, it is essential to include the LGNUM-ERP warehouse in the delivery output field catalog. Once this field is added, it will be available for creating a condition table and subsequently included in an access sequence. This enhancement ensures that the solution is restricted to Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) locations.

 

  1. Include field ZZLGNUM in communication structure KOMKBV2. This structure contains an Include that is provided for additional fields. Add the field with the name ZZLGNUM in Include KOMKBZ4.
  2. In structure KOMB in Include KOMBZ, you have to add the same field (same procedure as for item 1).
  3. In view V_T681F, you must create the same field with transaction SM31 under key B V2.
  4. 4. Program LVCOMU04 contains Perform USEREXIT_KOMKBV2_FILL. This Perform is in program RVCOMFZZ.
  5.  Modify the source code as follows: 

 

FORM_USEREXIT_KOMKBV2_FILL.

COM_KBV2-ZZLGNUM = COM_LIKP-LGNUM.

ENDFORM.

 

Step 2

 

Create an output condition table utilizing the Delivery Type and Warehouse Number (ZZLGNUM).

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Step 3

 

Create an access sequence using the previously created table to assign it to the condition type.

 

 

 

 

Step 4

Delivery Output Condition type creation.

 

 

Program – /SPE/DLV_IBGI_PROCESSING

Form Routine – ENTRY

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Step 5

 

Create a requirement to be used for the delivery output determination procedure.

Go to VOFM -> Requirements -> Output control

 

Create a new routine copying routine 1 code and add a wait for 2 seconds. This wait will make sure that the delivery is completed before the invoice is created. Otherwise, the invoice will be shown in the document flow before the PGI entry.

 

 

 

Maintain the Application as ‘V2’ and activate the routine.

 

Run the ‘RV80HGEN’ program using the SE38 transaction to activate the routine.

 

Note: This is client-specific activation.

 

 

Step 6

 

Create a new Output determination procedure with the newly created output type.

 

 

 

 

 

Assign the created requirement 901 to the procedure. This routine assignment ensures that invoice creation is delayed until after the PGI entry is created. It will make sure that the output type is assigned to the delivery only after the PGI is completed, preventing invoice generation from happening before the PGI entry.

 

 

Step 7

Assign the delivery output procedure to the Delivery type.

 

 

 

 

 

Step 8

 

Output condition maintain

Go to VV21 in order to maintain the output condition for the ZREA output type.

 

 

 

 

 

 

Output type ZREA will be available after the Goods Issue has been posted. Consequently, the invoice related to the post-goods issue will be created automatically.

 

Conclusion –

This configuration and the developments will ensure that invoice creation will be automated without major customization for the SAP standard solution.

 

Special thanks to the Altria Consulting team @Kalana_Chathuranga_Herath, @HasiniTW, @Nilupul_Rambukwelle, @Dushan_Sasmitha and @Ravindu_Herath.

entry.title: Defining Selection Variants for Shipments
entry.content:
Hi Community of SAP

You will explore how to define selection criteria for selecting deliveries to be assigned to a shipment.

You can select deliveries, for example, according to shipping point, due date, and status of the delivery. “Delivery” means return deliveries or incoming deliveries. this selection variant sets the criteria for deliveries to be added to the transport and makes it work faster

 

SAP IMG (SPRO)

Logistics Execution-Transportation-Shipments-Define Shipment Types

 

First, changes can be made to an existing shipment type or a new shipment type can be defined here

Enter the relevant shipment type with double click

After making the relevant configurations in the shipment type, go to the proposal section at the bottom

enter a variant name for the new variant to be created and click maintain

After clicking maintain, the following screen will open. here you can create a variant for the deliveries to be connected to the shipment. it can be created by giving data such as shipping point, loading point, loading date …

In addition, if “include deliveries in proceed shipment” is checked, the delivery to be connected to the shipment will be automatically assigned according to the selected criteria.

But if a condition is written behind the shipping exits, it will be assigned directly to the shipment without detecting it. for example; if the delivery is in credit blockage and a code is written that a delivery in credit blockage should not be assigned to shipping, it will not enter this code and will pass directly to the assigned side.

this credit blockage may cause problems by being noticed in the following processes. or if the delivery that is automatically assigned is pulled back to the not assgined lower part and assigned to the upper part again, the error written in the exit can only be noticed in that way and this will cause a big waste of time.

if “include deliveries in proceed shipment” is not checked, the delivery will appear below as unassigned. and can be assigned with user control

In addition, Ovrl Goods Mvt Sts and or transportation planning status can be included in the variant by giving an additional filter here.

if we want to make a field mandatory in the variant selection, click on the attributes button

description is entered and we can check which fields should be mandatory in the delivery selection screens.

When the shipment creation screen is checked again, it can be seen that the field selected in the selection variant is mandatory.

additionally created variants can be seen in the selection variant section of the VT01N input screen

You can examine the program of the created variant by entering the SAPLV56L related program at se38.

I would be happy to read your thoughts and comments, and if there are different comments and ways, write them in the comments.

 

Best Regards

entry.title: SAP SD Variant Pricing: Configuration and Implementation
entry.content:
Variant pricing in SAP SD provides a flexible pricing mechanism for configurable materials, allowing price determination based on material characteristics. This article outlines the configuration steps required to implement variant pricing effectively.

Advantages and Disadvantages of Variant Pricing

Advantages:

Enables dynamic pricing based on material variant configuration.

Reduces the effort needed to maintain existing variant pricing conditions.

 

Disadvantages:

Applicable only to configurable materials.

Requires a more complex setup compared to standard pricing.

 

Variant pricing is only applicable to configurable materials. The material must have a type of KMAT or a custom type derived from KMAT. Options of KMAT material type can be checked from OMS2.

 

 

You can check the material type via:

  • MARA-MTART field in the MARA table.
  • MM03 (Material Master) → Basic Data tab → Info button.

 

 

Also 0002 (Configuration) general item category group should be set for configurable materials.

 

“Material is configurable” checkbox should be set in MM01 → Basic Data 2.

 

The characteristic values those determines variant prices, can be set directly or copied from another characteristic (for example a characteristic determines component for production order). In this article, configuration of second scenario (Values are copied from another characteristic’s values) is shown.

 

Steps of Variant Pricing Configuration:

  • Create Characteristics in CT04

Use Transaction Code (Tcode) CT04 to create a characteristic.

Assign characteristic values matching the values from the referenced characteristic.

 

 

Under the Additional Data tab, set table name as SDCOM (SD and Variant Configuration Communication), field Name as VKOND (Variant Condition)

If values of characteristic are copied from another characteristic, check the “Not Ready for Input” option.

 

 

  • Create a Variant Class in CL01

Use Tcode CL01 to create a Class Type = 300 (Variants).

A variant class is created from CL01 by selecting 300 (variants) class type.

 

 

Assign the previously created characteristics to the class at “Char.” section.

Save the class.

 

 

  • Assign the Variant Class to the Material

Use Tcode MM02 and navigate to the Classification section and assign the created variant class.

Note: Alternatively, classes can also be assigned directly to material types in OMS2.

 

 

  • Define Dependency in CU01

The rules of the characteristic values ​​to be selected and their copying to other values ​​are determined through the dependencies.

Use Tcode CU01 to create a dependency.

Set Dependency Type as “Procedure”.

 

Define a dependency rule in the Dependency Editor to copy characteristic values to pricing characteristics. Dependency rule can be set from “Dependency editor” as shown in the image.

Save the dependency.

 

  • Create a Configuration Profile in CU41

Use Tcode CU41 to create a configuration profile for the material.

Double-click the profile.

 

 

Navigate to Dependency Assignments by clicking the button that marked in image.

 

 

Assign the previously created dependency.

 

Save the configuration profile.

 

  • Define Pricing Characteristics in VK30

Add pricing characteristic values to Variant Fields.

Ensure that these values align with the pricing structure.

 

 

 

  • Configure Pricing Procedure and Condition Records

 

Use V/03, V/04 to create or modify a condition table including variant (VARCOND) field.

Assign condition table to access sequence from V/07 by adding the new condition table to access.

 

 

Create a variant condition type from V/06.

Assign the new Access Sequence to this condition type.

 

 

Check the “Variant Condition” checkbox.

 

 

 

Add the variant condition to the pricing procedure from V/08.

 

 

Use VK11 to create condition records ensuring that, variant values match the pricing characteristic’s values and the values in VK30.

 

 

 

  • Testing the Pricing in Sales Order (VA01)

Create a sales order using VA01.

Assign characteristic values in Characteristic Value Assignment screen reaching by the button marked in the image belove.

 

 

 

After setting characteristic values, verify the price determination process in the conditions tab.

Confirm that pricing is correctly applied based on selected variant characteristics.

entry.title: SD Dynamic Product Proposal
entry.content:
What is a Dynamic Product Proposal?

Dynamic product proposal is a customization used to display products that can be sold to a specific customer as suggestions on the screen within a defined sales document type (such as a quote, contract, or order).

What Are the Advantages?

It plays a role in creating quick and easy orders for special customers.

It allows adding products to the order through additional material proposal or creating orders using a list of discounted products.

Spro menu

Defining the Customer Procedure for Product Proposal

The customer procedure field is assigned to the relevant field in the Sales tab of the customer master data in the SD screen. I will show this part later.

Defining the Document Procedure for Product Proposal

A procedure key is defined for the document types where the dynamic product proposal function is to be used.

Assign Document Procedure for Product Proposal to Sales Document Types

The product proposal document procedure key is assigned to the sales document type.

Maintain Table of Origin for Product Proposal

 This customization is where the source table maintenance is done, which determines how the product proposal will be executed.

 Customizations from A to E are source tables that already exist in the standard. This customization does not have a function on its own. It will be used as a separator in subsequent customizations.

Order History: It brings up the materials used in previously created orders as proposals.
Listing: By performing maintenance through transaction code VB01, the materials available for the customer are listed and then brought up as proposals on the sales document. If the listing method will be used, the Sales → Basic Functions → Dynamic Product Proposal Listing/Exclusion customizations should be made.
Item Proposal: Materials used in item proposal documents entered with transaction code VA51 are brought up as product proposals on the sales document.
Customer-Material Information: Materials entered for a specific customer and sales organization through transaction code VD51 are brought up as product proposals.
Exclusion: Materials that are entered as excluded for the customer using transaction code VA51 are brought up as product proposals. If the exclusion method will be used, the Sales → Basic Functions → Dynamic Product Proposal Listing/Exclusion customizations should be made.

Define Product Proposal Procedure and Determine Access Sequences

 The product proposal procedure can be customized to work either in the background or in the foreground. The access sequence for procedures is determined. The data source for the recommended materials is specified, and it is processed using the standard functions defined during the access sequence. For example, item proposals defined with transaction code VA51 are copied to the order document using the standard function SD_PP_PRODUCT_PROPOSAL.

  Procedure A00001 compares materials used in sales orders from the last 12 months based on the A-Sales Document date, and during access, it retrieves data using the SD_DPP_HISTORY function. The source table used is the Order History. The "A" indicator in the transaction area specifies that the materials returned by the function are directly written to the screen and brought up as proposals.

 

Other Usable Standard Functions

SD_DPP_CROSS_SELLING: Dynamischer Produktvorschlag: Cross-Selling Materialien ermittel

SD_DPP_CUSTOMER_MATERIAL_INFO: Dynamischer Produktvorschlag: Materialien aus Kunden-Info-Satz

SD_DPP_EXCLUSION: Dynamischer Produktvorschlag: Ausgeschlossene Materialien ermitt

SD_DPP_GET_BLOCK_FLAG: DP&P: Prüfung auf Sperre

SD_DPP_GET_BLOCK_FLAG_ADDRESS: DP&P: Prüfung auf Sperre Adressnummer

SD_DPP_HISTORY: Dynamischer Produktvorschlag: Auftragshistorie ermitteln

SD_DPP_LISTING: Dynamischer Produktvorschlag: Gelistete Materialien ermitteln

SD_DPP_PRODUCT_PROPOSAL: Dynamischer Produktvorschlag: Materialien aus Positionsvorschlag

SD_DPP_READ: Dynamischen Produktvorschlag Lesen

Maintain Procedure Determination (Online) for Product Proposal

 Assign the procedures we want to use in the SD area.

Maintain Procedure Determination (in Background) for Product Proposal

Assign the procedures we want to use in the SD area.

Important Tables for Dynamic Product Proposal

In addition to the VA51 transaction code, other sources can also be used as data sources.
If the sales history (previous orders) data is used as a source, it is possible to consider the daily, weekly, and monthly sales made by the customer before and create item proposals. Historical sales data is retrieved from the 'VBPK SALES DOCUMENT PRODUCT PROPOSAL HEADER' and 'VBPV SALES DOCUMENT PRODUCT PROPOSAL ITEM' tables. The update of these tables is done using the SDPV PRODUCT PROPOSAL GENERATION transaction code. To automate this process, it is possible to define a background job that runs periodically using the SDPVGEN program.

VBPK SALES DOCUMENT PRODUCT PROPOSAL HEADER

 

VBPV SALES DOCUMENT PRODUCT PROPOSAL ITEM

SE38-SDPVGEN

 

Sales Document Types Spro

The standard item proposal type 'PV' is used.

Document Type - D - Product Proposal

Transaction Flow Parameters

How to Create Item Proposals with Transaction Code VA51

Item proposals are executed with entry parameters in the SD fields.
By entering the description and validity date ranges, the relevant material and quantity are processed and saved.
The created item proposal is placed in the item proposal field in the customer's BP order tab and saved.

It operates based on the maintenance done in the "Product Proposal Customer Procedure (KNVV-PVKSM)" field in the Sales tab of the customer master data, under the FLCU01 - SD data role.

Go to VA01 for create order

We will receive a warning like this on the VA01 screen.

Then, we will bring up the item proposal by following the numbers from the toolbar above.

The system makes it easier for us here. If there are multiple item proposals, we can select the items, or if the customer order quantities vary, we can manually process them with the option to select without a quantity. If the quantities are the same, we can directly reflect the quantity defined in VA51 to the order.

 

I would be happy to read your thoughts and comments and if there are better ways to fulfil the requirement, please leave them in the comment section below.

 

Thanks so much

entry.title: Implementing and Debugging User Exits: Function Module in SAP Sales Order (VA01)
entry.content:
Implementing User Exits in SAP Sales Order (VA01)

Definition and Purpose
User exits are predefined enhancement points in SAP that allow developers to insert custom logic without modifying the standard code. They ensure system flexibility while maintaining compatibility with future updates.

Types of User Exits:
🔹Menu Exits – Add custom menu items for navigation (e.g., linking a custom report in the sales order menu).
🔹Function Module Exits – Insert custom validation or modify data before saving (e.g., validating sales order inputs).
🔹Screen Exits – Add custom fields to SAP screens (e.g., extending customer master data screens).

Implementation Example: Function Module
Using USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE, we enforce a minimum order quantity in VA01 to ensure data accuracy.

Read more to optimize your SAP workflow.

Implementing User Exits: Function

For this example, we want to create a sales document using VA01 transaction code, but we set the minimum quantity that the user input as 100.

  • Go to VA01 transaction code

  • Fill in the Order Type based on your requirement, but in this case we just fill in with OR or a standard order, then press Enter
  • So, our purpose is to make Order Quantity have a minimum value

If the user doesn't input the Order Quantity according to the minimum limit, the user will get a message.

  • Now we want to find the related user exit to add our logic, by clicking System > Status

  • Then double-click the Program (screen) name

  • We will jump into the SAPMV45A program

  • Press Display Object List
  1. Go to Subroutines and find the subroutine that is related to the user's exits by finding the subroutines that start with USEREXIT

  • Choose USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE

  • We will set our logic here

  • To find the spot that we can edit, Go to Edit >  Enhancements operations > Show Implicit Enhancement Options

  • Then press Enhancement

  • At the end of the form USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE right-click then press Enhancement operations > Create Implementation

  • Choose Code

  • Create new Implementation

  • Put this code inside the Enhancement Implementation.
ABAP
  data: lv_flag TYPE boolean.

   CLEAR lv_flag.
   IF sy-UNAME = ''. "fill in with your user name
     LOOP AT xvbap INTO DATA(ls_xvbap) WHERE updkz ne 'D'.
       IF ls_xvbap-KWMENG < '100.000'.
         MESSAGE 'The quantity must be higher than 100' TYPE 'I'.
         lv_flag = 'X'.
         exit.
       ENDIF.
     ENDLOOP.

     IF lv_flag = 'X'.
       leave to SCREEN sy-DYNNR.
     ENDIF.

   ENDIF.
  • Add debug in line

 

  • Click Direct processing
  • Input sales order OR

  • Add data, then click save

  • Abab debug will be displayed, the click continue’

  • Error message will be displayed. it means, user exit is developed successfully.

entry.title: Contact Person Creation in SAP S4 HANA: A Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
This blog post provides a step-by-step guide on how to create a contact person, link them to a customer, and understand the derivation of contact person number ranges. Additionally, it explores technical details like CVI mapping, relevant tables, and customization options using BADI.

Objective

The primary objective of this blog post is to help SAP S/4HANA users:

1. Create a contact person for a Business Partner.
2. Link the contact person to a customer.
3. Understand how contact person number ranges are derived.
4. Explore technical aspects like CVI mapping and customization options.

Create and Link Contact Person in S4 HANA

Create BP using "person" and Role "BUP001".
Contact person - 800000102 created.

Go to actual BP - 800000101 and navigate to the Relationship tab.

1. Select the relationship category FBUR001 (Contact Person).
2. Add the contact person (e.g., 800000102) and Maintain all necessary details such as department, function, and communication data.
3. Upon saving, the contact person (95) will be created and linked to the Business Partner.

Additionally we can find CVI map in Tcode- MDS_LINKS, where we can see Contact person  for BP- 800000102 created contact person 95 for customer.

Maintain partner Function for BP.
Navigate to the Sales Area Data and Partner Functions tab in the Business Partner (BP) transaction.

Contact person 95 is now linked with Customer master, shown below in table- KNVP & KNVK.

Finding CVI Mapping
To link a contact person to a customer in SAP S/4HANA, we can find the GUID from the table- BUT000  and reference it in the  table- CVI_CUST_CT_LINK. Here's how you can achieve this:

Understanding Contact Person Number Range
The contact person number (e.g., 95) is created based on the number range object “PARTNER” and the number range “AP”, which is hardcoded for this process.

For customization of the contact person creation process, implement the BADI: CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management (ARM): A Basic Guide
entry.content:
Efficient returns management is a critical aspect of modern business operations, directly impacting customer satisfaction, operational efficiency, and financial performance. Traditional SAP return processes required separate workflows for customer returns, supplier returns, and stock transfer returns, often leading to manual interventions, inefficiencies, and high processing costs.

With SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management (ARM), SAP has unified these workflows into an automated, structured, and integrated framework. This blog provides a detailed walkthrough of ARM, covering core concepts, process flows, key advantages, and step-by-step configuration.

1. What is SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management (ARM)?

SAP Advanced Returns Management (ARM) is a return management solution embedded within SAP S/4HANA, designed to streamline end-to-end return processes across various business scenarios.

1.1 Key Benefits of ARM

  • Unified return processing for customer returns, supplier returns, and stock transfer returns.
  • Automated document generation, reducing manual effort and errors.
  • Integration with SAP Quality Management (QM), Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), and Finance (FI/CO).
  • Dynamic follow-up actions based on return reasons and inspection results.
  • Enhanced visibility and tracking through SAP Fiori applications such as Manage Customer Returns.

 

2. Traditional SAP Returns Management vs. ARM

Key Differences Between Traditional Returns and ARM

Feature

Traditional SAP Returns

SAP S/4HANA ARM

Process Structure

Separate return processes for customer, supplier, and stock transfers

Unified, integrated return process

Document Flow

Requires manual handling

Automated follow-up document generation

Quality Management

Manual quality inspections

Automated QM integration with inspection lots

Tracking & Monitoring

Complex, manual tracking

Centralized tracking via Manage Customer Returns App

Automation

Manual document handling

Automated refund approvals and logistics execution

ARM reduces process complexity, eliminates redundancies, and improves end-to-end return cycle efficiency.

 

3. SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management (ARM) Processes

SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management operates through three primary return processes: customer returns, supplier returns, and stock transfer returns.

3.1 Customer Returns

Customer returns refer to the process initiated when a customer sends back a product for specific reasons. These returns can be classified as either planned or unplanned:

  • Planned Returns: Returns anticipated due to order errors, shipment issues, or product recalls.
  • Unplanned Returns: Returns initiated by the customer without prior notification or justification.

Customer Return Process Steps

  1. Creation of a Return Order (Return Sales Order)
  2. Generation of a Return Delivery Document (Return Delivery)
  3. Goods Receipt Processing (Goods Receipt)
  4. Quality Inspection and Follow-Up Actions (Quality Inspection & Follow-Up)
  5. Determination of Refund Amount and Execution of Customer Credit or Replacement (Refund or Replacement)

SAP Fiori's Manage Customer Returns application enables businesses to efficiently track and manage customer return orders.

 

ARM Configuration for Customer Returns

With the standard configuration complete, you’re ready to configure ARM for customer returns.

Here’s an overview of the most important steps:

➡️Activate ARM for return order types

You need to activate the sales document type to be used in ARM and assign subsequent document types for:

✔️Credit memo request

✔️Replacement order

✔️Delivery document

 

Step 1: Activate ARM for Return Order Types

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Activate Advanced Returns Management for Returns Order Types

✅Action:

✔️The CBAR sales document type is activated.

✔️Assigned subsequent document types.

 

Step 2: Activate ARM for CMR Order Types

📌Objective:

You need to activate the credit memo request sales document type to create a credit memo request during the refund process.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Activate Advanced Returns Management for CMR Order Types

✅Action:

✔️The GA2 sales document type is activated.

 

Step 3: Activate ARM for SDF Order Types

📌Objective:

The sales document type for replacement orders is used to create free-of-cost replacement orders in ARM.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Activate Advanced Returns Management for SDF Order Types

✅Action:

✔️The SD2 sales document type is activated.

 

Step 4: Configure, Activate, and Rename Follow-Up Activities

📌Objective:

You need to define logistics follow-up activities for processing return deliveries, which will drive the logistics flow of the return material.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Activate and Rename Follow-Up Activities

✅Action:

✔️Activate and rename follow-up activities used in ARM.

 

Step 5: Activate and Rename the Refund Control

📌Objective:

You need to activate refund control configuration, which will help in defining the controls on refund processing, such as:

✔️Process credit immediately

✔️No refund

✔️Refund through credit memo request processing

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Activate and Rename Follow-Up Activities

✅Action:

✔️Activate and rename refund control for an ARM return order.

 

Step 6: Define Return Reasons for Customer Returns

📌Objective:

You need to define return reasons for customer returns configuration, which will help in controlling the credit process (whether to give a refund or not) and other pricing elements.

📌Example:

✔️A restocking fee can be controlled based on the return reason.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Define Return Reasons for Customer Returns

✅Action:

✔️Several return reasons can be defined.

 

Step 7: Configure Default Values for Returns Refund Codes

📌Objective:

You need to configure default values for returns refund codes to control the automatic credit memo creation process for:

✔️Granting credit

✔️Rejecting credit based on inspection results provided by the quality team

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Configure Default Values for Returns Refund Codes

✅Action:

✔️Set up default refund codes to align with inspection results.

 

Step 8: Specify Follow-Up Document Types for Customer Returns

📌Objective:

You need to specify follow-up document types for customer returns to maintain:

✔️Automatic credit memo request processing

✔️Replacement orders

✔️Return stock/transfer orders

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Follow-Up Documents → Specify Storage Locations for Follow-Up Activities

✅Assigned Document Types:

✔️NB2 → Supplier returns

✔️UB2 → Intracompany returns to another plant

✔️NB2C → Intercompany returns to another plant

✔️SD2 → SDF replacements

✔️GA2 → Credit memo requests

 

Step 9: Configure Movement Type for Goods Receipt Posting

📌Objective:

You need to specify the movement types for immediate goods receipt posting into various stock types, such as:

✔️Free stock

✔️Blocked stock

✔️Quality inspection stock

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Follow-Up Documents → Specify Movement Types for Immediate Goods Receipts

✅Action:

✔️Maintain movement types for schedule line category AR.

 

Step 10: Specify Stock Type for Stock Transfers

📌Objective:

You need to specify the stock types, such as:

✔️Unrestricted stock

✔️Blocked stock

These stock types will be derived from the quality inspection result follow-up activity.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Follow-Up Documents → Specify Stock Types for Goods Receipts

✅Action:

✔️Maintain plant-specific stock types.

 

Step 11: Map ERP Delivery Type to EWM Delivery

📌Objective:

To replicate outbound and inbound deliveries in the EWM system, you’ll need to map ERP delivery types to EWM delivery types.

📌IMG Path:

➡️SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Document Types from ERP System to EWM

✅Action:

✔️Ensure proper mapping of delivery document types for ARM returns.

 

Step 12: Map ERP Item Categories to EWM Item Types

📌Objective:

You also need to map ERP item categories to EWM delivery item types to ensure correct logistics execution.

📌IMG Path:

➡️SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Item Types from ERP to EWM

✅Action:

✔️Configure item category mappings between ERP and EWM for ARM return processes.

 

3.2 Supplier Returns

The process of returning products to the supplier due to quality issues or other reasons.

Supplier Return Process Steps:

  1. Create a Return Purchase Order (Return Purchase Order)
  2. Execute Return Shipment (Return Outbound Delivery & Goods Issue)
  3. Conduct Quality Inspection at the Supplier's End (Supplier Inspection)
  4. Process Supplier Credit or Replacement (Credit/Replacements from Supplier)

 

ARM Configuration for Supplier Returns

This section describes the configuration settings required for the ARM return purchase order process for a plant.

We assume that other Customizing settings (such as organizational structure, pricing, and output control) are already in place. You also need to maintain the configuration of document types across purchasing, shipping, inventory management, and invoice verification.

In addition to these configurations, you also need to perform the following mappings between embedded EWM and SAP S/4HANA (the ERP system).

 

Step: Set Up EWM for Supplier Returns

📌Objective:

To map the ERP delivery type and item category with the EWM document type and item type.

📌IMG Path:

➡️SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Document Types

✅Action:

✔️Map ERP item categories with EWM item types via:

➡️SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Item Types from ERP to EWM

 

3.3 Stock Transfer Returns

Stock transfer returns refer to return processes between different plants within the company.

Stock Transfer Return Process Steps:

  1. Create a Return Stock Transfer Order (Return Stock Transfer Order)
  2. Execute Shipment and Goods Issue (Outbound Delivery & Goods Issue)
  3. Goods Receipt and Quality Inspection at the Receiving Plant (Goods Receipt & Quality Inspection)
  4. Perform Relevant Follow-Up Actions (Follow-Up Actions)

 

ARM Configuration for Stock Transfer Returns

With the standard configuration complete, you’re ready to configure ARM for return stock transfer orders. Below is an overview of the most important steps:

 

Step 1: Activate the ARM Return Stock Transfer Order Document Type

📌Objective:

You can activate ARM for return stock transfer order document types.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Returns Order → Advances Returns Management → Activate Advanced Returns Management for Purchase Order Types

✅Action:

✔️UB2 and NB2C purchase order types are activated for ARM.

 

Step 2: Define the Number Range for Advanced Returns Process IDs

📌Objective:

Define a number range for process IDs to enable process monitoring.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Returns Order → Advances Returns Management → Define Number Range for Advanced Returns Process IDs

✅Action:

✔️The number range interval must be 01.

 

Step 3: Configure the Usage of Delivery Types for Store Returns

📌Objective:

Assign purchasing document category type F (purchase order) for document types UB2 and NB2C, as well as the plant (SPlt).

📌IMG Path:

➡️Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Returns Order → Store Return / Return Plant to Plant

✅Action:

✔️Configure delivery document types for store returns.

 

Step 4: Configure the Automatic Creation of Outbound Deliveries

📌Objective:

Set up automatic creation of outbound deliveries for return stock transfer orders.

📌IMG Path & Actions:

✔️Activate automatic replenishment deliveries & CRM billing

➡️Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Setup Stock Transport Order → Activate Automatic Delivery Creation and CRM Billing

✔️Activate or deactivate automatic outbound deliveries for stock transfer orders (per document type and shipping point)

➡️Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Setup Stock Transport Order → Activate Automatic Delivery Creation for PO Type and Shipping Point

✔️Define profiles for creating and processing deliveries

➡️Logistics Execution → Shipping → Worklists → Maintain Parameters for Creating Deliveries

(SAP provides profile STOA, but custom profiles can be created.)

 

Step 5: Map ERP Delivery Type to EWM Delivery

📌Objective:

To replicate outbound and inbound deliveries in the EWM system, you need to map ERP delivery types to EWM delivery types.

📌IMG Path:

➡️SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Document Types from ERP System to EWM

✅Action:

✔️Ensure proper mapping of delivery document types for ARM return processes.

 

Step 6: Map ERP Item Categories to EWM Item Types

📌Objective:

To ensure correct logistics execution, you need to map ERP item categories to EWM delivery item types.

📌IMG Path:

➡️SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Item Types from ERP to EWM

✅Action:

✔️Configure item category mappings between ERP and EWM for ARM stock transfer returns.

 

4. Integration with Quality Management

SAP S/4HANA’s Advanced Returns Management (ARM) solution is seamlessly integrated into customer returns, supplier returns, and stock transfer returns.

SAP has developed a straightforward approach to document material inspection in the ARM process, allowing users to enter inspection codes and comments.

Furthermore, ARM can be integrated with the Quality Management (QM) module in SAP S/4HANA for companies requiring detailed quality inspections to support quality assurance and process improvements in manufacturing industries.

How Quality Management is Integrated in ARM:

✔️Use an inspection lot to enter data for material inspection in ARM.

✔️Use a quality notification to:

  • Create a return order
  • Display a return order
  • Display a return order overview

This section will explore how quality management is integrated into returns processing, including its role in inspections and key configuration activities.

 

4.1 Quality Management in Returns Processing

In ARM, material inspection can be performed to accept or reject returned products.

  • Companies can choose to integrate ARM with Quality Management (QM) for automatic inspection lot creation.
  • The quality team inspects the product to determine the next steps, initiating a quality notification process for further analysis.
  • This helps companies diagnose product defects and make precise quality improvements.

 

Material Inspection Process in ARM

✔️A material inspection code can be entered at the time of return order creation.

✔️The inspection process is fast and optimized for retail and wholesale environments.

✔️A material inspection code can also be entered in the warehouse upon goods receipt for a return delivery.

Required Inspection Data in ARM:

To complete a material inspection, the following information must be recorded:

✔️Inspection code

✔️Inspection comments (long text)

✔️Material inspector details

✔️External document attachment

✔️Inspection date

When an inspection occurs at a customer location, the inspection results are typically entered at the time of return order creation.

📌To enter inspection results in the warehouse:

➡️SAP Easy Access → Logistics → Central Functions → Advanced Returns Management → Material Inspection → Enter Material Inspection in Warehouse

➡️Transaction Code: MSR_INSPWH

 

Key Fields in the Inspection Process

✔️Inspection Lot

  • The system updates the inspection lot number when activated.
  • Ad hoc inspections can be created by clicking the Create icon.

✔️Partial Lot

  • A unique number within the corresponding inspection lot.
  • Useful for batch splitting on the delivery note.

✔️Inspection Code

  • Defines the quality of the returned material as determined during inspection.

✔️Comment

  • Allows users to enter comments related to the inspection process.

✔️Inspection Date

  • Specifies the date when inspection was performed or recorded.

✔️Inspector

  • Identifies the person responsible for the inspection.

✔️Inspected Quantity

  • Defines the quantity of the inspected item or split item.

 

Conclusion

SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management (ARM) provides a powerful, automated, and fully integrated solution for managing customer returns, supplier returns, and stock transfer returns efficiently.

In this blog, we covered the key configuration steps and main processes involved in ARM. However, this content serves as a summary, and further details may be required based on specific business needs.

For more in-depth insights or customized solutions, feel free to reach out to us!

📌Your feedback and questions are always welcome!

Thank you for reading! 😊🚀

entry.title: Automatic Invoice Posting of Forwarding Agent against Shipment Cost Document
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

The following process will shed light on How to post Automatic Invoice Posting of Forwarding Agent for Shipment Cost Document.


Problem Statement:

The problem is that invoices of forwarding agents are being posted manually through journal voucher, in which, shipment cost number/shipment number is being entered manually in the narration or in a text field. Due to this, manual reconciliation is being conducted between Shipping Cost Document and Forwarding Agent Invoice as there is no synchronization between these documents and duplicate invoice is being posted along with payments disburse to vendors.

Solution:

To overcome the above problem the following configuration to be completed to post Automatic Invoice Posting of Forwarding Agent against Shipment Cost Document.

Step-1:               Map Vendor Partner Role IP (Invoicing Party) with Freight Carrier Account Group.

Step-2:               Define Item Category for Shipment Cost type by following path:

Step-3:              Define Item Category for Shipment Cost type as per your business requirements.

Step-4:              Assign Purchasing Data with respective Transportation Planning Plant and Plant by following path:

Step-5:         Purchase Organization, Purchasing Group to be mapped with Transportation Planning point, shipment cost type and Plant.

Step-6:         Assign respective Cost Element with respective GL account for respective company code by following path.

Step-7:         Condition type FT00 to be added in respective Shipment Cost Pricing Procedure and maintain with respective access sequence in transaction code – TK11.

Step-8:         Below these options to be ticked in respective Freight Carrier Vendor.

Step-9:         ERS output/pricing conditions to be maintained for respective forwarding agent.

Step-10:      After configuring/maintaining above settings/conditions ERS Service PO and Service Entry Sheet will be automatically created at the time of saving of Shipment Cost Document and display under “Service Procurement” tab of Shipment Cost Document. Also, Shipment Number displayed under “Purchase Order History” tab of Purchase Order.

Step-11:         After above steps, go to transaction MRRL and post the ERS invoice or schedule a batch job for program “RMMR1MRS” to avoid manual intervention to post the ERS invoice.

Step-12:         Now go to transaction FBL1N and check respective Freight Vendor Account to check posting/amount of Freight Vendor.

To Conclude:

Once the above steps will be executed successfully the business will get the correct account balance of freight vendor to avoid any dispute in the future related to duplicate invoicing or payment. It will also reduce the efforts of manual reconciliation.

Thanks for your interest! Please share feedback, comments or suggestions below.

Best Regards,
Neeraj Jain
SAP Technical Manager in HCL Technologies Limited

entry.title: SAP Make-to-Order Insights
entry.content:
Today we will learn about the Make to order process used in SAP, the blog will give you an understanding of the business process and the configuration steps.

Strategies which businesses follow to produce goods are as follows:

Make to Order (MTO): The product is not manufactured in advance, Product is produced once the order is received, like Cars, Trucks, Bus’s etc.

Make to Stock (MTS): The product is manufactured in advance, it is produced in mass and fast moving in nature, such as consumer products, pharmaceuticals, household items, etc. 

 

 

Among these two production strategies we will discuss the Make to Order (MTO) business process in this blog.

Make to Order (MTO) Production Strategy:

"Make to order" refers to a production strategy where a company only manufactures a product once a customer places an order, meaning they don't produce goods in advance and stockpile inventory; this approach prioritizes customization and flexibility, allowing for tailored products based on individual customer needs, while minimizing the risk of overproduction and associated inventory costs, but often results in longer lead times for customers as production begins after the order is received.

Example- Cars, Trucks, bus’s etc.

Benefits:

  1. Reduce Inventory cost as production starts after order, saving on storage space of product.
  2. Increase customer satisfaction as they get what they want, Leads to higher satisfaction and loyalty.
  3. Greater flexibility as companies can adapt to changing market demands and quickly introduce new product variations based on customer feedback.
  4. Less waste as no overproduction.

Challenges:

  1. Potential for higher costs as it will be produced for special customers.
  2. Efficient production planning to meet customer deadlines.
  3. Longer lead time as production starts after receiving order. 

When to use?

  1. High production customization, when customer requires unique features or options on their products.
  2. Fluctuating demand: If customer demand is unpredictable and varies significantly, make to order can prevent overstocking. 
  3. High-value products: For expensive items minimizing inventory is crucial. 

Process flow for Make to Order:

Standard Configuration guide to activate MTO:

  1. Item category group: 0001/0002
  2. Item Category: TAK
  3. Schedule line category: CP
  4. MRP Type: ND/PD
  5. Special stock indicator: E
  6. Item category determination: OR + 0001+ Blank + Blank =TAK
  7. Strategy group:020
  8. Requirement Class: 040
  9. Requirement type: KE

Pre-requisites:

  •       In Material master settings that need to be carried out are as follow:
  • In Sales: Sales org. 2 screen enter Item Category group= 0001 (MTO) / 0002 for Configurable material.
  • In MRP1 screen, MRP type= PD/ND (No planning) & MRP controller = 101
  • In MRP3 screen, Strategy Group= 20 (Make to order/No assembly order).
  • In MRP3 screen, Availability check= 01 (Daily requirement).
  • Depending on business requirement you can create or use standard Sales document type, Item category & Schedule line category.

    Process Steps:

    • Create sales order

    Note: Costing for MTO process depends on Sales order, enter EK02 condition manually.

              
    • After creating sales order, we need to check whether the requirement has transferred to MRP using MD04.
    • Requirements planning in (MRP) is a manufacturing process control that includes production planning, scheduling, and stock requirement, enter transaction code: MD02, enter material, plant, MRP control parameters & execute it.
             
    • Convert planned order to Production order using MD04, Choose -> Prod. Order (Convert planned order into production order).
             
    • Release the Production order by clicking on flag and save the Prod. Order.
    • Withdrawing the Material for the Production Order (System creates a reservation for the relevant material components when you make a production order) using MIGO.
    • Confirm the production order using CO11N.
    • Create goods receipt against sales order using MIGO, Enter Movement type as 101.
    • Check the stock overview using MMBE.
    • The schedule lines will be confirmed in sales order, therefore create delivery & post goods issue.
    • Once PGI is done create Billing document.

Expected errors and their prospect solutions are as follow:

  • Error: Sales order does not trigger production order

Prospect solution: Assign correct requirement type to the item category and Ensure requirement class is defined.

  • Error: MRP does not generate planned orders.

Prospect solution: Check strategy group in material master and assign the correct requirement type.

  • Error: Production order does not consume sales order stock.

Prospect solution: Set special stock indicator as” E” in MRP 4 view of material master and ensure MRP settings align with MTO requirements.

  • Error: Sales order confirmation ignores component availability (ATP Not Considering Component Lead Times).

Prospect solution: Check ATP scope in OVZ9 and maintain proper AATP settings and ensure component lead times in material master.

Conclusion: Make-to-Order (MTO) process, stock can be customized, manufacturing can only begin only after receiving a confirmed customer order. Its primary advantages are lower inventory costs, greater customer satisfaction, enhanced production planning, and resource allocation efficiency.

entry.title: Managing Multiple Addresses in SAP S/4HANA For BP & Customer Master
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA, the Business Partner (BP) model introduces a more efficient way to manage multiple addresses for customers and vendors. Unlike SAP ECC, where each customer (KNA1) or vendor (LFA1) had only one address, SAP S/4HANA allows multiple addresses within a single BP record.

This eliminates the need to create duplicate customer or vendor records for different shipping, billing, or ordering locations. By activating the Multiple Address Handling function, businesses can link multiple addresses to a single BP, reducing data redundancy and improving system efficiency in Sales and Distribution (SD).

To leverage multiple address handling, follow these steps to activate the required business functions:

STEP 1: ACTIVATE BUSINESS FUNCTIONS

  • Access Transaction SFW5: Enter tcode SFW5 in the SAP command field and press Enter.
  • Locate the Business Functions:
    In the Switch Framework, navigate to the Enterprise Business Functions. Search for and select the following functions:
    BPCUSTOMER_MULTIPLE_ADDRESSES
    Q2C_MULTIPLE_BP_ADDRESSES

    BPCUSTOMER_MULTIPLE_ADDRESSES → General multiple address functionality for Business Partners.
    Q2C_MULTIPLE_BP_ADDRESSES → Makes sure these multiple addresses work correctly in the sales process (quotes, orders, deliveries, etc.)



  • Activate the Functions:
    Check the boxes next to each function and Click the Activate Changes button to enable them.

Status is now activated.


If don’t have changes access to SFW5 then go to Tcode- SCC4 and check authorization and make the changes accordingly.

STEP 2: 
Go to the Address Overview screen and add two address details within the validity range. Once the addresses are added in BP, assign them to the relevant address usages by double-clicking on the delivery address under Address Usages and selecting the appropriate address from the pop-up window.

 

Both the address has been added under delivery address.

Note : Now, in BP, the "Address-Dependent Order Data" field will be displayed for Orders, Shipping, and Invoices.

STEP 3: ADDRESS DETERMINATION

After activating the necessary business functions, the "Address Determination" option will become available in the partner procedure for the Sales Order header. Add BBP003 & BBP004 in Address determination filed for Ship to and Bill to party.

Tcode- VOPAN (for Sales document Header)


Below setup ensures that address determination functions correctly for partner functions like BBP003 & BBP004 in the sales process.

(i) Define Transactions for Address Determination:

Navigate to:
Cross-Application Components → SAP Business Partner → Business Partner → Address Determination → Define Transactions

(ii) Define Address Types
Navigate to:
Cross-Application Components → SAP Business Partner → Business Partner → Address Determination → Define Address Types

Ensure that multiple address usage is enabled for relevant address types.

(iii) Assign Transactions to Address Types
Navigate to:
Cross-Application Components → SAP Business Partner → Business Partner → Address Determination → Define Address Types

Link the defined transactions to the corresponding address types to ensure proper address assignment.

STEP 4: Create Sales order.

Now, a pop-up window will appear for selecting the Ship-To party address.

For, Bill to party, there is only one address has been assigned in BP address overview, which will get determined in SO.

STEP 5: TABLE RELATION

BUT020 : Contains the main addresses for Business Partners. Linked to BUT021_FS through the BP number (PARTNER field)

Table BUT021_FS: This table in SAP is used to store addresses for a Business Partner (BP) with a validity date.

Field XDFADU: This is a yes/no (indicator) field that tells SAP which address is the "main" address during a specific time period. If it's marked "X", that means this is the standard address for the business partner at that time

Note : If "Address Description", "Partner Description", and "Default Address" are maintained in BP, then KNVP (ADRNR, KNREF, DEFPA) will be updated.

Address description helps users recognize the address without going into full details.

Note: 

If the address pop-up appears for the Sold-To Party and needs to be restricted, we can set a default address in the Partner Determination Procedure. This ensures the system automatically selects the correct address, preventing unnecessary prompts while still allowing multiple addresses for the Ship-To Party in Sales Orders.
